title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams releases 'Our City. Our Beer.' variety pack with beers inspired by Derrick White, Garrett Crochet, Julian Edelman, and Zdeno Chára.
The pack features White Ale, Crochet's Pale Ale, Julian Edel-Brau Lager, and Big Z Pilsner, each with unique flavors.
A launch party at Banners Kitchen & Tap in Boston on April 4 will feature the athletes serving their themed beers.

title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Dave McGillivray plans to run his 54th Boston Marathon with his children Max, Luke, Elle, and astronaut Sunita Williams.
The 2026 Boston Marathon holds special meaning for McGillivray, involving family and honoring late Susan Hurley's philanthropic legacy.
Sunita Williams, a retired astronaut, joins McGillivray's charity team, marking her return after running a marathon in space.

title: Jayson Tatum, Derrick White sound off on ‘ridiculous’ Jaylen Brown ejection
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected before halftime during the Celtics' game against the Spurs, sparking teammate criticism.
Jayson Tatum and Derrick White both agreed the first technical foul was justified, but condemned the second ruling.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla supported Brown's reaction to the ejection, emphasizing its impact on the nationally televised game.

title: He’s running Boston to honor of his mother’s adventurous spirit
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: James Hebert, 62, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his late mother, Janice.
Janice Hebert inspired James with her adventurous spirit, undertaking activities like hang-gliding and skydiving.
James Hebert will wear a lilac-colored shirt during the race, representing his mother's favorite flower.

title: Authorities look into possible vandalism of expensive geotubes on Nantucket
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Authorities investigate vandalism of geotubes on Nantucket's Sconset Bluff, with knife-like cuts causing damage.
Sconset Beach Preservation Fund offers $10,000 reward for information amid ongoing police investigation.
Winter storm further damages geotubes, increasing erosion risk to Sconset Bluff and Sankaty Head Lighthouse.

title: Immigration officials ordered to release Marlborough 14-year-old detained apparently as ‘hostage’ to target her father, Rep. Trahan says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ordered the release of a 14-year-old girl in Marlborough detained by ICE targeting her father.
Rep. Lori Trahan claims the girl was used as a 'hostage' to coerce her father into deportation.
DHS disputes the claim, stating the girl was rescued from gang members and held for her safety.

title: Mass. woman among victims in Utah triple homicide; fundraiser launched for husband
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Easthampton resident Natalie Graves, 34, was killed while hiking in Utah in a triple homicide on March 4.
Suspect in custody is believed to have murdered another woman the same day before targeting Graves and her aunt.
A GoFundMe for Graves's husband, Taylor, raised over $38,000 to manage expenses related to her death.

title: Here’s what to know about the new $1 billion Gillette headquarters planned for Fort Point
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Procter & Gamble plans to build a new $1 billion Gillette headquarters at 232 A St. in South Boston.
The current South Boston facility will transform into residential, retail, lab, and office spaces after manufacturing moves to Andover.
The nearby Andover facility will accommodate manufacturing, packaging, and fulfillment operations for Gillette's blades and razors.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots signed Dre'Mont Jones, previously of the Ravens, to improve their pass-rush ranked 26th in 2025.
Jones recorded 15 quarterback hits with the Ravens, leading the team during his nine games last season.
Dre'Mont Jones is now with his fifth NFL team, the Patriots, following stints with the Broncos, Seahawks, Titans, and Ravens.

title: Seamark at Encore Boston to close, less than a month after workers unionize
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Seamark Seafood & Cocktails at Encore Boston Harbor to close permanently on March 29, citing economic challenges.
56 workers to be laid off; closure follows successful unionization vote joining UNITE HERE Local 26 last month.
Union alleges closure is retaliatory, claims Seamark engaged in anti-union efforts throughout the unionization process.
